ai_generated_debt.issue_character: The debt is isolated to a single subsystem (SketchHandlers): three private single-line pass-through delegates that add no logic and could be inlined at their single call sites in routeSketchRequest.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase scores well on most AI-generated-debt signals: comments explain WHY not WHAT, naming is domain-specific, and there is no debug-logging or null-check overengineering. The one clear pattern is three pass-through wrappers (handleFullSketch, handleRelationSketch, handleFullDependencySketch) in SketchHandlers that each forward to a more specific method with no added logic — a classic AI refactoring artefact. Individually trivial, but as a cluster of three they represent a distinct naming-layer habit that harms readability. Score sits at 82: solid overall, pulled down from the high 80s by the concentrated pass-through cluster.

issues.summary: Three private single-line pass-through delegates in SketchHandlers add a naming layer with no logic
evidence: handleFullSketch (line 105): body is exactly `handleFullSketchForType(exchange, lemma, RelationType.SURFACE);`
handleRelationSketch (line 109): body is exactly `handleRelationQueryForPattern(exchange, lemma, relation, RelationType.SURFACE);`
All three are called from a single site in routeSketchRequest; none add auth, logging, metrics, or caching
issues.suggestion: Inline the three wrappers at their call sites in routeSketchRequest: replace the `handleFullSketch(exchange, lemma)` call with `handleFullSketchForType(exchange, lemma, RelationType.SURFACE)`, `handleRelationSketch(exchange, lemma, relation)` with `handleRelationQueryForPattern(exchange, lemma, relation, RelationType.SURFACE)`, and `handleFullDependencySketch(exchange, lemma)` with `handleDepSketch(exchange, lemma)`. Delete the three wrapper methods.

root_causes: Incremental extraction refactoring left single-call private delegates that were never collapsed back into their call site
